reasoning: Query asks for cherry blossom spots in Tohoku but the tool fetches generic Akita spots (castle ruins, Lake Tazawa, volcano, fireworks) with no sakura-specific filtering. The intent is season+region specific; the response ignores the sakura constraint entirely and returns unrelated attractions.
missing_data: Spots tagged sakura_meisho_100 in Tohoku (Kakunodate, Hirosaki Castle, Kitakami Tenshochi). No seasonal hint or sakura kind tag present in shown results.
improvement_hint: Filter by kinds containing sakura_meisho_100 or apply a seasonal/topic filter when query intent is clearly cherry-blossom; surface those results first before general spots.

reasoning: get_local_specialty returns 24 Niigata items led by the famous sake brands (越乃寒梅, 八海山, 久保田) with QIDs, descriptions, and authorities. Covers the user's intent well. Minor gap: response includes non-sake items (edamame GI) mixed in, slightly diluting focus.
missing_data: Physical brewery visit addresses or tasting-tour links would help planners; some items lack English name.
improvement_hint: Add a sake_brewery category filter or rank sake_brand/sake_brewery kind_tags first when query clearly targets sake breweries.

reasoning: Query asks specifically for ski resorts with onsen in Hakuba. The response returns Hakuba Happo-One (correct, Olympic ski resort) but then drifts to a UN Tourism news article, Hakuba Oike lake, and an art museum. No onsen spots appear in the visible results, so the thermal spring constraint is unaddressed.
missing_data: Onsen ryokan in Hakuba (e.g. Hakuba Sanso, Mimizuku-no-Yu), onsen kind tag, cross-reference to get_hotels for onsen_ryokan near Hakuba.
improvement_hint: When query combines ski_resort + onsen, include hotels layer (onsen_ryokan) and filter spots by both ski_resort and onsen kinds together.

reasoning: Query is Kansai autumn foliage but tool fetches generic Kyoto spots. Results show Nijo Castle, To-ji, Nagaokakyō — all notable but not foliage-specific. No momiji or koyo kind tags are surfaced. Query also says Kansai (multi-prefecture) but only Kyoto is fetched.
missing_data: Spots tagged with momiji/koyo kinds; multi-prefecture Kansai coverage (Nara, Osaka, Shiga) for autumn foliage; Tofuku-ji, Eikan-do, Rurikoin which are canonical Kyoto momiji spots.
improvement_hint: Implement a seasonal intent classifier that filters or boosts autumn-foliage kinds; expand to full Kansai region (fan-out across multiple prefectures) when query says Kansai.

reasoning: Query asks for famous cherry blossom spots in Kansai (Arabic). Tool fetches Nara spots and the first result is Yoshino (sakura_meisho_100, prominence 1.1, Must See) — exactly correct. Other Nara spots follow. Limited to one prefecture but Yoshino is the premier Kansai sakura site. Good intent match.
missing_data: Other Kansai prefectures (Osaka Mint, Kyoto Maruyama Park) not covered; query says Kansai not just Nara.
improvement_hint: Fan out to all Kansai prefectures and rank by sakura_meisho_100 kind tag when sakura intent is detected.

reasoning: Query asks for stargazing spots on remote Okinawa islands. Response returns Okinawa prefecture spots but leads with Nakagusuku Castle, Tamaudun (mausoleum), then Kumejima (relevant — remote island). No dark_sky or stargazing kind tag appears. The island constraint and stargazing intent are both largely unaddressed.
missing_data: dark_sky kind-tagged spots; Iriomote, Ishigaki, Taketomi, Yonaguni (remote islands with low light pollution); stargazing-specific venues.
improvement_hint: Add dark_sky_park kind tag to relevant Okinawa islands; surface island kind tags alongside low crowd_band when stargazing intent detected.

reasoning: Query asks for rural Hokkaido onsen accommodation (Thai). Tool returns get_hotels for Hokkaido — leads with Nihon Hito-yu wo Mamoru Kai certified onsen ryokan (Chinaionsen, Ginkonyu, Takachidake Onsen, Asahidake Onsen) — exactly rural hidden-spring places. The intent is well served. ANA Holiday Inn appears lower (urban, less relevant) but does not dominate.
missing_data: Coordinates missing on hito_yu_kai entries; no English names for most ryokan.
improvement_hint: Enrich hito_yu_kai entries with geocoordinates and English names; rank confirmed rural onsen_ryokan before urban hotels when rural constraint is present.

reasoning: Query asks for Seto Inland Sea islands (Vietnamese). Tool fetches Kagawa prefecture spots, returning Ritsurin Garden, Manno Lake, Marugame Castle — all mainland Kagawa, not islands. Naoshima, Shodoshima, Teshima (canonical Setouchi islands) are absent from visible results.
missing_data: Island-kind spots in Setouchi: Naoshima, Shodoshima, Teshima, Oshima; multi-prefecture Setouchi coverage (Hiroshima, Okayama, Ehime side islands).
improvement_hint: When query targets a sea/archipelago region, filter or prioritize island kind tag across relevant prefectures; Setouchi should fan out to at least Kagawa+Hiroshima+Okayama.

reasoning: Query asks for indigo dyeing workshops in Tokushima. The response returns METI craft records: 阿波正藍しじら織 (Awa Hon-ai Shijira Weave, indigo-related) and Awa Washi paper. The craft data correctly covers Tokushima crafts but identifies textile products rather than workshop venues. Otani Ware (ceramics) is less relevant. see_also returns Tebajima island — not directly relevant to workshops.
missing_data: Actual workshop/experience venues for indigo dyeing (藍染体験); specific studio addresses or tour operators.
improvement_hint: Add experience_venue or workshop kind tag to craft-experience locations; link METI craft records to associated venue spots where available.

reasoning: Query asks for Buddhist temple lodgings (Shukubo) on Mt. Koya (German). Tool returns Wakayama hotels, leading with multiple Koyasan Shukubo Association certified temples (Annyoin, Ichijoin, Ekoin, Kitamuroin, Kumagaiji). Excellent intent match — exactly the right data source. Descriptions are in Japanese only.
missing_data: English names for shukubo; coordinates absent for all shukubo entries; prices and availability info.
improvement_hint: Add English translations to shukubo descriptions; geocode Koyasan shukubo entries; this use case works extremely well.

reasoning: Query asks for samurai districts outside Kyoto. search_area('武家屋敷') returns 153 matches covering Shimane, Tokyo, Fukui, Kakunodate (Akita) — a good geographic spread. Kakunodate is prominently featured with rich description. Shimane Buke Yashiki leads (Matsue, not Kyoto). Excellent coverage for this intent.
missing_data: Chiran (Kagoshima), Kitsuki (Oita) samurai districts could strengthen diversity; some entries lack coordinates.
improvement_hint: Filter out Tokyo results when 'outside major cities' is implied; boost preservation_district + buke_yashiki kind combination.

reasoning: Query asks for Akita summer festivals (Indonesian). get_festivals returns only 3 items, none of which is the iconic summer festival: Akita Kanto Matsuri (lantern festival, early August) is completely absent. Items returned are intangible cultural properties (Dainichido Bugaku, Kakunodate Festival) with no dates. The summer constraint is unmet.
missing_data: 秋田竿燈まつり (Kanto Matsuri, August) — one of Tohoku's three great summer festivals; Omagari Fireworks (August, already in spots data). Date filtering for summer would help.
improvement_hint: Add Akita Kanto Matsuri to festival data; implement month/season filtering in get_festivals; scrape summer festival schedules from Akita tourism board.

reasoning: Query asks for small fishing port towns in the San'in region (Korean). Tool fetches Shimane spots returning Lake Shinji, Matsue Castle, Iwami Ginzan Silver Mine, Mt. Sanbe — none of which are fishing port towns. The fishing village / port town intent is completely unaddressed.
missing_data: Fishing port towns: Kaga Onsen coastal area, Uminoshima (温泉津), Ōnancho fishing villages, Masuda coast; fishing_port or fishing_village kind tag.
improvement_hint: Add fishing_port and coastal_town kind tags; when query intent includes fishing village/harbor, filter or boost those kinds.

reasoning: Query asks where to experience washi paper making in Gifu. get_local_specialty returns 8 craft items; 美濃和紙 (Mino Washi) should be top but the first result shown is Mino Ware (ceramics), and 飛騨春慶 (lacquer), 南木曽ろくろ細工 (lathe work) appear before washi. The washi craft (Mino Washi) is likely present but not visible in the 4000-char truncation.
missing_data: 美濃和紙 METI designation and workshop experience venues (Udatsu Washi Museum, Mino Washi Village); experience booking info.
improvement_hint: When query targets a specific craft, rank that craft's kind tag first in results; add a washi_experience venue kind to link craft records to workshop locations.

reasoning: Query asks for fishing villages on the Noto Peninsula (Spanish). Tool fetches Ishikawa spots returning Kanazawa Castle, Kenroku-en, Oyama Shrine — all Kanazawa city attractions, not Noto Peninsula fishing villages at all. The Noto Peninsula geographic constraint and fishing village intent are both completely ignored.
missing_data: Noto Peninsula fishing villages: Wajima, Suzu, Noto-jima, Sozushi, Hegura Island; municipality filter for Noto should be used.
improvement_hint: When query specifies a sub-regional area (e.g. Noto Peninsula), use municipality-level filtering; add fishing_village/coastal_town kind tags for Noto area spots.

reasoning: Query asks for lesser-known sand dunes beyond Tottori. search_area('砂丘') returns 91 results covering Tottori (expected to appear), Onjuku Beach (Chiba), Ishikari Museum (Hokkaido), Fukiage-hama (Kagoshima). Good geographic diversity. Tottori appears first but that's acceptable. The response serves the intent well.
missing_data: Sakata sand dunes (Chiba), Uchimio sand dunes (Aomori), Kyushu examples; some results lack coordinates.
wrong_data: Ishikari Local Museum is tagged sand_dune but is a museum about the dune, not the dune itself — minor categorization issue.
improvement_hint: Disambiguate museum-about-dune vs actual dune in kind tags; add more regional sand dune entries from official natural monument registries.

reasoning: Query asks for Sanin region traditional crafts (Chinese). Tool queries Shimane only (half of Sanin). Returns 4 items: 石見焼, 石州和紙, 雲州そろばん, 出雲石燈ろう — all accurate and well-described in Chinese. However Tottori prefecture (the other Sanin prefecture) is not included, missing Inshū Washi and other Tottori crafts.
missing_data: Tottori prefecture crafts (因州和紙, 弓浜絣); the query says 山陰地区 (Sanin region = Shimane + Tottori) but only Shimane is fetched.
improvement_hint: When query specifies a geographic region (山陰), apply region fan-out to include both Shimane and Tottori prefectures automatically.

reasoning: Query asks specifically for lavender fields beyond Furano in Hokkaido. The tool fetches generic Hokkaido spots: Showa-shinzan volcano, Sapporo Clock Tower, Matsumae Castle — none lavender-related. The lavender kind tag exists in the system but is not used here. Completely fails the specific intent.
missing_data: Lavender fields: Farm Tomita (Furano, expected to be noted as the baseline), Hokuryu Himawari-no-Sato, Nakafurano lavender farms; lavender_field kind tag.
improvement_hint: Filter or boost lavender_field kind tag when lavender intent is detected; this is exactly the type of case where kind-tag routing should kick in.

reasoning: Query in Filipino asks for traditional towns in Kyushu (interpreted as historic townscape/streetscape). Tool fetches Oita spots: Usuki Castle, Usa Shrine, Oka Castle — historic but not traditional townscape districts. The komachi/machinami/preservation_district intent is unaddressed. Oita is also only one Kyushu prefecture.
missing_data: Traditional townscape districts in Kyushu: Chiran samurai town (Kagoshima), Hita Mameda (Oita), Yufuin (Oita), Yanagawa; multi-prefecture Kyushu coverage.

reasoning: Query asks for kominka (traditional farmhouse) accommodation in Hokuriku. Tool fetches Ishikawa hotels returning onsen ryokan (Shiramine, Nakamiyū) and 'Student housing 1' (OSM noise), AB Hotel. No kominka accommodation appears. The kominka lodging intent is unmet; the Hokuriku scope (Ishikawa only, not Toyama or Fukui) is also partial.

reasoning: Query asks for mountain shrines on the Kii Peninsula. get_japan_heritage for Wakayama returns Kongōbu-ji (Koyasan), Kumano Kodō, Kumano Sanzan — these are the canonical mountain-worship sacred sites of the Kii Peninsula, with UNESCO WHS status noted. Excellent intent match with high specificity.
missing_data: Nachi Taisha, Hongu Taisha, Hayatama Taisha individually listed (they appear as part of Kumano Sanzan but individual links could help); Niu Tsuhime Shrine.
improvement_hint: Surface individual Kumano Sanzan shrine entries alongside the aggregate record for agents that need specific venue-level data.

reasoning: Query asks for summer fireworks festivals along the Sea of Japan coast. Tool fetches Niigata festivals (129 items). Within the visible results, Nagaoka Fireworks Festival (Aug 2-3, one of Japan's top 3) appears — exactly right. However top results are 芝桜まつり (moss phlox spring festival) and ヒメサユリ祭り (lily festival) which are spring/summer but not fireworks and not Sea of Japan themed. The fireworks/hanabi filter is absent.
missing_data: Other Sea of Japan coast fireworks: Kashiwazaki Umi no Festival (Niigata), Sakata Fireworks (Yamagata), Akita-Omagari; cross-prefecture Sea of Japan coverage.
improvement_hint: When fireworks intent detected, filter by hanabi/fireworks kind tags or event type; implement coast/region filtering for Sea of Japan query.

reasoning: Query asks for traditional bamboo crafts in Oita. get_local_specialty returns 2 items: 博多織 (Hakata weave, cross-listed in Oita) and 別府竹細工 (Beppu Bamboo Crafts) — the latter is exactly the canonical Oita bamboo craft. Well-described in English with production areas. Hakata Ori is textile/weaving, slightly off-topic but legitimately listed for Oita.
missing_data: Beppu craft experience venues (Beppu Traditional Bamboo Crafts Museum); only 2 items total is sparse — additional Oita crafts like 日田下駄 could be included.
improvement_hint: Link craft records to workshop/museum venues via see_also; add more Oita craft items if METI data is incomplete.

reasoning: Query asks for rural Shikoku cycling routes. Tool fetches Ehime spots. The response includes Ōmishima island (cycling_route kind tag) — relevant — but leads with castles (Ōzu, Yuzuki, Uwajima). The cycling_route kind tag exists but cycling-specific results are buried below general Ehime spots. No route-specific information (distance, difficulty, trailheads) is provided.
missing_data: Shimanami Kaidō route spot (the premier Shikoku cycling route); other Shikoku prefectures (Kochi, Kagawa) for multi-prefecture cycling coverage; route-level data.
improvement_hint: When cycling intent detected, filter and prioritize cycling_route kind-tagged spots; Shimanami Kaidō should rank first for Shikoku cycling queries.

reasoning: Query asks where to see kabuki outside Tokyo and Kyoto. get_traditional_arts returns 183 items starting with Bunraku (Osaka — relevant but not kabuki), then kabuki (UNESCO inscription, national level — no venue), then Chakkirako dance, Dainichido Bugaku. The kabuki item has no venue or geographic specificity. Items are not filtered for kabuki or geographic constraint.
missing_data: Regional kabuki venues: Kanamaru-za (Kotohira, Kagawa — oldest existing kabuki theater), Uchiko-za (Ehime), Zashikikawaroko (Akita). No venue-specific results with addresses or performance schedules.
improvement_hint: Add kabuki_theater kind tag to historic theater venues; when kabuki query with geographic exclusion, surface venue-level spots with address rather than just national-level UNESCO records.
